LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-02-2020 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH WU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH WU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THERE IS A STRONG POLICY IN FAVOR OF LIBERAL ALLOWANCE OF AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS. (MESLER V. BRAGG MGMT. CO. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 290, 296.) IF DEFENDANT WISHES TO CHALLENGE THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE NEW CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE GROUND THAT THEY ARE TIME-BARRED, THE APPROPRIATE MECHANISM FOR DOING SO IS BY WAY OF A DEMURRER OR DISPOSITIVE MOTION. (SEE CALIFORNIA CASUALTY GEN. INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1985) 173 CAL.APP.3D 274, 281 ["THE PREFERABLE PRACTICE WOULD BE TO PERMIT THE AMENDMENT AND ALLOW THE PARTIES TO TEST ITS LEGAL SUFFICIENCY BY DEMURRER, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS OR OTHER APPROPRIATE PROCEEDINGS."].) PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR# 6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH WU'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made by the parties. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Plaintiff Elizabeth Wu's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. There is a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments to pleadings. (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) If Defendant wishes to challenge the sufficiency of the new causes of action on the ground that they are time-barred, the appropriate mechanism for doing so is by way of a demurrer or dispositive motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].)

Plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS)
